# Ralphglasses Bootstrap

Use this skill for first-time setup or when a repo has lost its known-good runtime profile.

## Default workflow

1. Read `ralph:///bootstrap/checklist` and `ralph:///runtime/health` before touching config so bootstrap work starts from the live contract instead of guesswork.
2. Run `ralphglasses_doctor` to confirm provider CLIs, paths, and workspace prerequisites.
3. Use `ralphglasses_validate` and `ralphglasses_config_schema` to inspect config drift before writing anything.
4. Apply or inspect reusable setup profiles with `ralphglasses_firstboot_profile`.
5. Use `ralphglasses_repo_scaffold` when the repo is missing baseline files or MCP wiring.
6. Check `ralphglasses_server_health` after setup to confirm the control plane is healthy.
7. Hand off to `ralphglasses-operator` only for the interactive root `firstboot` wizard or TUI-native steps.

## When to use it

- First-time workstation or repo bring-up
- Repairing a broken `.ralphrc` or missing baseline config
- Applying named firstboot profiles
- Re-establishing MCP/server readiness after drift

## Guardrails

- Prefer the profile tool over manual file edits when a supported profile exists.
- Do not use the interactive wizard for automation-friendly steps that already have MCP parity.
- Discovery tools are here only to orient bootstrap work, not to replace the dedicated discovery skill.
